productCenter: A local-first personal media instrument that feels authored, strange, tactile and alive: weird personal-web energy translated into a coherent phone-first operating surface, not a retro website skin.
requiredTraits: Personal specificity must be visible before platform structure.
Navigation should invite curiosity and spatial discovery without hiding core actions.
Media, typography and chrome may collide, but action contracts and phone legibility remain exact.
Visual richness should come from composition, state and user material rather than new backend subsystems.
Surfaces should feel handmade and consequential, never generic SaaS or a themed dashboard.
RIVER, FOCUS and FIELD remain distinct physical reading modes.
rejectDrift: fake social proof or engagement furniture
new coordination, sync or schema systems without executable product need

sources.title: Neocities browse: top sites
sources.notes: Broad current personal-web sample: authored homepages, shrines, journals, link pages, badges and intentionally nonuniform layouts.

sources.title: Neocities activity feed
sources.notes: Sites behave like rooms that keep changing rather than polished launch artifacts.

sources.title: Neocities social discovery
sources.notes: Discovery emerges from human links and follow graphs rather than an opaque recommendation feed.

sources.title: Neocities random discovery
sources.notes: A useful anti-convergence sample because adjacent sites frequently disagree about layout, density, tone and navigation.

sources.title: Neocities project premise
sources.notes: The platform premise favors direct authorship, inspectable source and individuality over standardized social profiles.

sources.title: IndieWeb principles
sources.notes: UX and design should outrank protocols and schema; longevity and plurality matter; above all have fun.

sources.title: IndieWeb personal site
sources.notes: The site is a home controlled by its author, not merely a profile inside someone else's product.

sources.title: GeoCities Gallery
sources.notes: Archived neighborhood pages show maximal personal specificity, visual layering, topic obsession and direct linking.

sources.title: One Terabyte of Kilobyte Age
sources.notes: Large visual archive useful for separating recurring personal-web composition from literal retro ornament.

sources.title: Wiby small-web search
sources.notes: Discovery is centered on handmade pages and low-commercial-pressure documents rather than optimized platforms.

sources.title: Nekoweb browse
sources.notes: A younger neighboring personal-web ecosystem mixing old visual vocabulary with contemporary subculture and tools.

sources.title: Sadgrl online resources
sources.notes: Treats webmaking as learnable craft and encourages reuse, remix, linking and explicit authorship.

sources.title: 32-Bit Cafe
sources.notes: Contemporary personal-web community with prompts and resources that make authorship social without flattening sites into one template.

sources.title: MelonLand
sources.notes: Personal-web community that foregrounds exploration, neighborhoods, expressive pages and tool sharing.

sources.title: Web Design Museum early web collection
sources.notes: Historical references help identify durable interaction patterns while avoiding shallow nostalgia cosplay.

sources.title: Cameron's World
sources.notes: A navigable collage proves maximal visual abundance can still have spatial rhythm and intentional pathways.

sources.title: Motherfucking Website
sources.notes: Counterweight: personality does not require a framework, giant system, or ornamental complexity.

sources.title: Brutalist Websites archive
sources.notes: Useful for contemporary harshness, physical hierarchy and anti-SaaS composition without copying period chrome.

sources.title: The HTML Review
sources.notes: Shows browser-native interaction and authored structure producing meaning without app-platform conventions.

sources.title: Now page movement
sources.notes: Small recurring personal updates can create intimacy without engagement mechanics or social proof.
